cases.title: Testing-plan §3 policy-dependent rows require FR-25 effective resolution at check-in and session close
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 coverage blocks for GPS validation, Attendance status assignment, and Close-time absent finalization apply.
Hierarchy fixtures exist: institution default, faculty, course, and class-section policies with distinct field values at each level.
Open class session exists for the target section with enrolled students and valid QR token.
steps: Invoke M06 resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionScheduledStart) and record resolved presentWindowMinutes, lateWindowMinutes, gpsRequired, and gpsRadiusMeters with per-field source levels.
Submit student check-in within present window and verify Present assignment uses resolved presentWindowMinutes (AC-11 row).
Submit check-in with gpsRequired true and missing GPS payload; verify rejection uses resolved gpsRequired from effective policy (AC-09 row).
Close session without successful check-in for a pending student; verify Absent finalization applies per resolved effective policy at close (AC-12 row).
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix cross-walk confirms FR-25 resolution feeds all three testing-plan policy-dependent rows: check-in status windows, GPS requirement enforcement, and close-time absent processing each consume the same single effective policy resolved via BR-20 precedence.

cases.title: M06 resolveEffectivePolicy merges per-field precedence section over course over faculty over institution
preconditions: Real DB transaction boundary for attendance_policies table.
Institution policy: presentWindowMinutes 10, lateWindowMinutes 10, gpsRequired false.
Faculty policy: presentWindowMinutes 12 (other fields undefined).
Course policy: lateWindowMinutes 18, gpsRequired true, gpsRadiusMeters 120 (presentWindowMinutes undefined).
Class-section policy: presentWindowMinutes 20 only (other fields undefined).
Target classSectionId belongs to the configured hierarchy.
steps: Seed four active AttendancePolicy rows at Institution, Faculty, Course, and ClassSection scopes.
Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionTimestamp) from M06 Policy Engine.
Assert presentWindowMinutes 20 sourced from ClassSection.
Assert lateWindowMinutes 18 sourced from Course (section and faculty undefined).
Assert gpsRequired true and gpsRadiusMeters 120 sourced from Course.
Assert institution defaults apply for fields undefined at all child levels (e.g. manualEditWindowHours, absenceThresholdPercent).
cases.expected: BR-20 produces one merged effective policy: each field uses the most specific defined value (section > course > faculty > institution); undefined fields fall back to parent levels without requiring duplicate configuration at every scope.

cases.title: M06 resolves identical effective policy at check-in timestamp and session close for same class section
preconditions: Class section has section-scoped policy with presentWindowMinutes 15, lateWindowMinutes 15, and autoCloseEnabled true.
Open class session exists with known scheduledStart and classSectionId.
M04 check-in pipeline and M03 session-close hook both call resolveEffectivePolicy.
steps: Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, checkInTimestamp) during an open session and capture resolved policy snapshot.
Process a successful check-in and verify Present/Late decision uses the captured present and late window values.
Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionCloseTimestamp) from M03 close processing.
Compare close-time snapshot to check-in snapshot for presentWindowMinutes, lateWindowMinutes, autoCloseEnabled, and manualEditWindowHours.
Verify auto-absent finalization (BR-13) and auto-close evaluation (BR-21) reference the close-time resolved policy.
cases.expected: FR-25 mandates resolution at both check-in and session close; for the same section and session, effective policy fields are consistent unless an admin mutates policy between events; close processing applies absent rules per the resolved close-time policy.

cases.title: Session close beforeState Open transitions to Closed with Absent rows per resolved effective policy
preconditions: Open class session with enrolled students; some have Present/Late, others remain Pending.
Effective policy resolved for section defines excused-absence handling and absent finalization rules.
One student has Excused status before close; another has Manual Present.
steps: Capture beforeState: session state Open, roster counts, and resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, now).
Invoke M03 closeSession(sessionId, lecturerActor) triggering absent finalization hook.
Capture afterState: session state Closed, attendance_records statuses.
Verify Pending students without success become Absent per BR-13 and effective policy.
Verify Excused and Manual Present students are not overwritten to Absent.
cases.expected: beforeState Open → afterState Closed; absent finalization honors FR-25 effective policy at close time; Excused and Manual Present exceptions preserved per BR-13.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins assigns Present or Late based on section-resolved presentWindowMinutes overriding institution default
preconditions: Institution default presentWindowMinutes 10; section-scoped override presentWindowMinutes 25 via POST /v1/policies.
Lecturer has opened session; valid qrToken is available.
Two authenticated enrolled students have no prior success for the session.
Session scheduledStart is known; test client timestamps are relative to scheduledStart.
steps: Student A calls POST /v1/check-ins with clientTimestamp at scheduledStart + 12 minutes (within institution default but after section override would still be Present at +12 if window is 25).
Assert HTTP 200, data.outcome Success, data.attendanceStatus Present (section window 25 applies, not institution 10).
Student B calls POST /v1/check-ins with clientTimestamp at scheduledStart + 22 minutes (after institution present window 10 but within section window 25).
Assert HTTP 200 with data.attendanceStatus Present for Student B confirming section override governs outcome.
cases.expected: Effective policy resolution at check-in uses section presentWindowMinutes 25; students checking in between minute 10 and 25 receive Present rather than Late; HTTP 200 success envelope includes correct attendanceStatus per FR-25 and BR-20.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance roster reflects policy-resolved Present and Late counts
preconditions: Section-scoped policy sets presentWindowMinutes 15 and lateWindowMinutes 15 from session scheduledStart.
Open session with valid qrToken; three enrolled students submit check-in at present window, late window, and remain pending.
Lecturer bearer token is assigned to the session section.
steps: Process check-ins via POST /v1/check-ins at scheduledStart + 5 min (Present), + 20 min (Late), and leave third student pending.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization.
Inspect HTTP 200 envelope: data.counts.present, data.counts.late, data.counts.pending.
Verify data.rows contain attendanceStatus Present, Late, and Pending with checkInMethod QR where applicable.
Confirm counts align with effective policy window boundaries not institution defaults.
cases.expected: Live roster payload per FR-19 reflects FR-25 effective policy: counts and row statuses match Present/Late assignments derived from section-resolved windows; rejectedAttempts and pending counts are accurate in standard API envelope.

cases.title: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close finalizes Absent per effective policy at close time
preconditions: Open session with five enrolled students: two Present, one Late, two Pending.
Effective policy for section defines standard absent finalization; no Excused students among pending.
Lecturer assigned to section is authenticated.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close with Lecturer Authorization and Idempotency-Key.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.state Closed and data.summary.absent reflecting two previously Pending students.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and verify pending students now show attendanceStatus Absent.
Confirm Present and Late students retain prior statuses.
cases.expected: Session close HTTP 200 response includes absent summary count driven by FR-25 effective policy at close; pending enrolled students without success become Absent per BR-13; existing Present/Late records unchanged.

cases.title: WF-03 student check-in happy path uses resolved effective policy for Present assignment
preconditions: WF-01 complete: section, enrollments, sessions Scheduled; optional section policy override presentWindowMinutes 15.
Lecturer completes WF-02: POST /class-sessions/{id}/open transitions session to Open with QR.
Authenticated enrolled student has no prior success; check-in occurs within resolved present window.
steps: Resolve effective policy for class section at session scheduled start and record presentWindowMinutes.
Student scans QR and POST /v1/check-ins succeeds within present window per WF-03.
Verify WF-03 checkpoint: status assignment Present or Late is derived from effective policy windows.
Confirm CheckInAttempt outcome Success and AttendanceRecord method QR are persisted.
cases.expected: WF-03 primary lifecycle completes with Present status determined by FR-25 effective policy resolution at check-in time; AC-11 checkpoint satisfied via BR-20 precedence chain.

cases.title: Per-field fallback when section policy defines only presentWindow and inherits lateWindow and GPS from course level
preconditions: Institution default: presentWindowMinutes 10, lateWindowMinutes 10, gpsRequired false.
Course policy: lateWindowMinutes 20, gpsRequired true, gpsRadiusMeters 100 (presentWindowMinutes undefined).
Section policy: presentWindowMinutes 30 only (lateWindowMinutes and gpsRequired undefined).
Open session exists for the section with room coordinates configured.
steps: Resolve effective policy and confirm presentWindowMinutes 30 (section), lateWindowMinutes 20 (course), gpsRequired true (course).
Student checks in at scheduledStart + 25 minutes; assert Present (within section present window 30).
Student checks in at scheduledStart + 35 minutes without GPS; assert HTTP 422 with error.code GpsRequired (inherited from course).
Student checks in at scheduledStart + 35 minutes with valid in-radius GPS; assert Late (after present window 30, within late window 20 ending at +50).
cases.expected: Alternate precedence branch: section overrides present window only; late window and GPS requirement inherit from course per BR-20 per-field fallback; check-in outcomes reflect merged effective policy not any single scope row alone.

cases.title: Section policy PATCH changes subsequent check-in resolution without affecting prior attendance records
preconditions: Open session with section policy presentWindowMinutes 15.
Student A successfully checked in Present under 15-minute window.
AcademicAdmin is authenticated; audit query available.
steps: Record beforeState: resolveEffectivePolicy shows presentWindowMinutes 15.
AcademicAdmin PATCH /v1/policies/{policyId} changing presentWindowMinutes to 30.
Record afterState: resolveEffectivePolicy shows presentWindowMinutes 30.
Student B checks in at scheduledStart + 20 minutes; assert Present (would have been Late under beforeState).
Verify Student A AttendanceRecord remains Present unchanged.
Query audit logs for policy mutation with old and new presentWindowMinutes values.
cases.expected: Critical policy configuration change updates FR-25 resolution for subsequent check-ins immediately; prior attendance records are immutable; audit trail captures policy mutation per BR-22.

cases.title: POST /v1/auth/login as AcademicAdmin then GET /v1/me before verifying effective policy drives check-in setup
preconditions: AcademicAdmin test account exists with institution scope.
Class section with hierarchy policies seeded; open session fixture pending policy verification.
steps: Call POST /v1/auth/login with AcademicAdmin credentials.
Assert HTTP 200 with accessToken and AcademicAdmin role in data envelope.
Call GET /v1/me with Bearer token; assert institution scope and AcademicAdmin role.
Configure or verify section policy override via authorized admin APIs.
Open session and confirm subsequent POST /v1/check-ins outcomes align with resolved effective policy for enrolled student.
cases.expected: Auth gate per FR-15 and FR-36 succeeds; admin actor can verify and exercise FR-25 effective policy resolution in end-to-end check-in flow.

cases.title: Student denied AttendancePolicy.read; Lecturer limited to effective-only read for assigned section
preconditions: Student and Lecturer test accounts exist with valid bearer tokens.
Attendance policies exist at institution and section levels.
Lecturer is assigned to target class section; Student is enrolled in a different section.
steps: Call GET /v1/policies or effective-policy endpoint as Student.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden; no policy configuration or precedence details leaked.
Call effective-policy read as Lecturer for assigned section; assert HTTP 200 with resolved values only (no raw multi-scope policy list).
Call effective-policy read as Lecturer for unassigned section; assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope.
Call GET /v1/policies list as Lecturer; assert Deny or scoped effective-only response per permission matrix.
cases.expected: RBAC enforces AttendancePolicy.read Deny for Student; Lecturer receives effective-only resolution for assigned sections without exposing full policy administration APIs; cross-section access denied per BR-19.

cases.title: Institution-only default policy resolves when no faculty, course, or section overrides exist
preconditions: Class section exists with no faculty, course, or section AttendancePolicy rows.
Single institution-scoped policy defines presentWindowMinutes 10, lateWindowMinutes 15, gpsRequired false.
Open session scheduled for the section.
steps: Call resolveEffectivePolicy(classSectionId, sessionTimestamp).
Assert all behavioral fields match institution policy values.
Submit check-in at scheduledStart + 12 minutes; verify Present.
Submit check-in at scheduledStart + 12 minutes for second student after advancing clock to + 18 minutes; verify Late.
Confirm no error or null policy returned at any resolution point.
cases.expected: FR-25 resolves institution defaults as the effective policy when no more specific overrides exist; check-in Present/Late boundaries follow institution presentWindowMinutes 10 and lateWindowMinutes 15 without requiring child-scope rows.

cases.title: Parallel check-in requests resolve consistent effective policy snapshot for same session
preconditions: Open session with section-scoped policy presentWindowMinutes 15.
Ten authenticated enrolled students with no prior success.
Integration harness supports parallel HTTP or service calls.
steps: Fire ten concurrent POST /v1/check-ins (or M04 submitCheckIn) requests within present window.
Collect each response attendanceStatus and implied policy window used.
Query CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord tables for all ten students.
Verify all successful outcomes used presentWindowMinutes 15 consistently; no split-brain Late assignments within present window.
cases.expected: Concurrent check-ins during stable policy configuration all resolve the same effective policy; no race causes inconsistent window application across students in the same session.

cases.title: PolicyResolutionSummary accordion displays effective precedence chain and per-field source levels
preconditions: AcademicAdmin logged in on desktop Chromium at /admin/policies create or edit route.
Institution, course, and section policies exist with different presentWindowMinutes values.
PolicyResolutionSummary (DC-09) renders for target class section.
steps: Navigate to FRM-07 policy form for a class section with partial override.
Expand PolicyResolutionSummary accordion sections for present window, late window, GPS required, and manual-edit window.
Verify each field shows resolved effective value and source level badge (section, course, faculty, or institution).
Confirm precedence order displayed as section → course → faculty → institution.
Change section present window value and observe summary preview updates before save.
cases.expected: DC-09 browser journey satisfies FR-25 explainability: admins see single effective policy with BR-20 precedence and per-field provenance before and during configuration.

cases.title: Lecturer live roster page shows Present and Late badges driven by effective policy windows
preconditions: Lecturer logged in on desktop Chromium.
Open session with section policy presentWindowMinutes 15; students have checked in within present and late windows.
Lecturer navigates to session roster page for assigned section.
steps: Open live roster view for open session and verify SessionStatusBadge shows Open.
Confirm DataTable columns include student name, attendance status badge, check-in time, and method.
Verify students who checked in within 15-minute present window display Present badge.
Verify students who checked in after present window display Late badge.
Observe roster counts update without full page reload when a new student checks in within policy window.
cases.expected: Lecturer UI reflects FR-25 effective policy at runtime: Present/Late badges and roster counts match policy-resolved windows; live updates align with GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance data per FR-19.
